class.name: Speech
class.brief_description: Multiplayer voice chat node that manages audio capture, compression, transmission, and playback.
class.description: Speech is the high-level VoIP node for multiplayer voice communication. It captures microphone input via [SpeechProcessor], compresses audio with Opus, and transmits packets to remote peers. On the receiving side, it manages per-player jitter buffers, decodes incoming voice packets, and feeds decoded audio into [AudioStreamGenerator] playback streams. It also provides adaptive jitter buffering with configurable speedup and slowdown thresholds to maintain smooth playback.

method.name: add_player_audio
method.description: Registers an audio stream player for the given [param player_id]. The [param audio_stream_player] will be used to play back decoded voice audio received from that remote peer. A jitter buffer and [SpeechDecoder] are created automatically for the player.

method.name: attempt_to_feed_stream
method.description: Decodes packets from the [param jitter_buffer] and pushes audio frames into the [param audio_stream_player]'s playback stream. Uses [param decoder] to decompress Opus packets, [param skip_count] to drop stale packets, and [param playback_stats] to record buffer metrics. The [param player_dict] holds per-player state, and [param process_delta_time] is the frame delta used for timing calculations.

method.name: calc_playback_ring_buffer_length
method.description: Calculates the playback ring buffer length in frames for the given [AudioStreamGenerator], based on its mix rate and buffer length. Returns the computed size as a power-of-two aligned value.

method.name: clear_all_player_audio
method.description: Removes all registered player audio entries, freeing their decoders, jitter buffers, and playback state.

method.name: clear_skipped_audio_packets
method.description: Resets the skipped audio packet counter to zero.

method.name: copy_and_clear_buffers
method.description: Copies all pending compressed input audio buffers into an [Array] and clears the internal buffer queue. Each element in the returned array is a [PackedByteArray] containing one Opus-compressed audio packet.

method.name: decompress_buffer
method.description: Decodes an Opus-compressed audio buffer using the given [param decoder]. Reads [param read_size] bytes from [param read_array], decodes them to PCM, and writes the resulting stereo sample pairs into [param write_array]. Returns the filled [PackedVector2Array].

method.name: end_recording
method.description: Stops capturing audio from the microphone and halts the speech processor. Returns [code]true[/code] if recording was successfully stopped.

method.name: get_playback_stats
method.description: Returns a [Dictionary] of playback statistics for all registered players, merged with the capture statistics in [param speech_stat]. Each player entry includes jitter buffer size, playback ring buffer usage, and timing information.

method.name: get_skipped_audio_packets
method.description: Returns the number of audio packets that were skipped due to buffer overflow since the last call to [method clear_skipped_audio_packets].

method.name: get_speech_decoder
method.description: Creates and returns a new [SpeechDecoder] instance configured for the current audio settings. Each remote peer should have its own decoder to maintain independent Opus decoder state.

method.name: get_stats
method.description: Returns a [Dictionary] containing capture-side statistics from the [SpeechProcessor], including sample rates, buffer sizes, and audio capture metrics.

method.name: on_received_audio_packet
method.description: Handles an incoming Opus-compressed voice [param packet] from the remote peer identified by [param peer_id]. The [param sequence_id] is used for ordering packets in the jitter buffer. The packet is queued for decoding and playback on the next process frame.

method.name: remove_player_audio
method.description: Unregisters the audio stream player and associated resources for the given [param player_id], removing the player's jitter buffer and decoder.

method.name: set_audio_input_stream_player
method.description: Assigns an [AudioStreamPlayer] node as the microphone input source. The [param player] must be an [AudioStreamPlayer] with an audio capture effect attached. Returns [code]true[/code] if the player was successfully assigned.

method.name: set_streaming_bus
method.description: Sets the audio bus name used for capturing microphone input. The [param bus] must have an [AudioEffectCapture] attached for audio capture to work.

method.name: start_recording
method.description: Begins capturing audio from the microphone and starts the speech processor. Returns [code]true[/code] if recording was successfully started.

members.member: The maximum allowed delay in seconds before the playback buffer is considered too far behind and corrective action is taken.
If [code]true[/code], enables verbose debug logging for voice chat operations including jitter buffer state and packet statistics.
The jitter buffer size threshold below which playback pitch is reduced to allow the buffer to refill and prevent underruns.
The jitter buffer size threshold above which playback pitch is increased to drain excess buffered packets and reduce latency.
The maximum number of packets that can be held in a player's jitter buffer. Packets arriving when the buffer is full are discarded.
The pitch scale applied to the audio stream player when the jitter buffer exceeds [member JITTER_BUFFER_SPEEDUP], causing playback to speed up and drain the buffer.
The default pitch scale applied to the audio stream player during normal playback when the jitter buffer is within acceptable bounds.
A pre-allocated silent audio packet used to fill the playback stream when no voice data is available, preventing audio underruns.
The number of voice packets received during the current process frame. Reset each frame during processing.
The length of the playback ring buffer in frames, computed from the [AudioStreamGenerator] settings. Used to track buffer fill level during playback.
A [Dictionary] mapping peer IDs to their playback state, including the audio stream player, [SpeechDecoder], jitter buffer, and [PlaybackStats] for each connected player.
Holds the most recently decoded uncompressed audio samples as stereo pairs. Can be used to access raw PCM data for visualization or analysis.
